response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack web and TypeScript engineer with a long career, but he fails the most critical hard requirements for this role. There is no mention of Japanese language proficiency anywhere on the resume, which is listed as a non-negotiable requirement. His work is overwhelmingly web-oriented (TypeScript, Node.js, Ember.js), with only superficial C/C++ experience from early-career roles and no evidence of Linux kernel-level work, embedded firmware development, or any security domain knowledge relevant to LAN-switch products (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, etc.). The role requires a fundamentally different technical profile.
strengths: 30+ years of software development experience across diverse domains including some early embedded and low-level work
Exposure to Linux environments and low-latency systems programming at Sociomantic
C and C++ listed in skills with early-career usage at National Instruments writing components for a C IDE
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency mentioned anywhere — an absolute hard requirement for this position
Zero demonstrated knowledge of network security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, encryption key exchange) or Layer-2/3 switch networking
No Linux kernel investigation or source-code modification experience; no embedded firmware or chip-vendor SDK work
